tracks.title: AI Fundamentals
tracks.summary: Learn what models, tokens, context windows, reasoning, and multimodal workflows actually mean in practice.
outcomes: Understand the core language of modern AI systems
Know when to use chat, vision, or structured outputs
Build a mental model for prompts, inputs, and limits

tracks.title: Prompt Engineering Studio
tracks.summary: Practice writing prompts that create reliable outputs across summarization, planning, teaching, and marketing tasks.
outcomes: Write stronger system, user, and task prompts
Use examples, constraints, and output formats effectively
Debug weak prompts and iterate with intent

tracks.title: Agents and Automation
tracks.summary: Move from one-off prompts to repeatable agent workflows, tool use, and human-in-the-loop automations.
outcomes: Understand how agents plan, call tools, and recover from failures
Design workflows that mix prompts with real business systems
Choose where humans should review or approve outcomes

tracks.title: Evaluation and Safety
tracks.summary: Learn how to judge output quality, create simple evals, and reduce risk when AI is used in real decisions.
outcomes: Create practical output checks and evaluation rubrics
Spot hallucinations, overreach, and fragile prompt patterns
Use safer workflows for learning, support, and operations

resources.title: AI Glossary: Core Concepts
resources.summary: A plain-language walkthrough of models, embeddings, agents, fine-tuning, evals, reasoning, and tool use.
resources.bestFor: People who are new to AI vocabulary and want a low-friction starting point.

resources.title: Prompt Patterns Playbook
resources.summary: Reusable prompt structures for planning, extraction, role prompting, critique loops, teaching, and transformations.
resources.bestFor: Builders who want practical prompt scaffolds they can adapt immediately.

resources.title: Agent Architecture Notes
resources.summary: Explains memory, tool routing, error recovery, approvals, and how small agents differ from larger orchestrated systems.
resources.bestFor: People designing AI workflows that need reliability, not just clever outputs.

resources.title: Classroom AI Starter Kit
resources.summary: A bundle of lesson ideas, rubric prompts, feedback helpers, and classroom-safe guidelines for teachers and tutors.
resources.bestFor: Educators who want to use AI responsibly for planning and feedback.

resources.title: Output Evaluation Checklist
resources.summary: A practical rubric for checking accuracy, completeness, structure, tone, and risk before AI outputs go live.
resources.bestFor: Teams that want a repeatable review layer before using outputs externally.

resources.title: Build Your First AI Workflow
resources.summary: A start-to-finish walkthrough for turning one useful prompt into a repeatable workflow with inputs, checks, and outputs.
resources.bestFor: Anyone who has used AI casually and wants to build something durable next.
